rows.description: Item de impensis Coll. et nota Domus to Mr Hutchins & wife & sister. 1708
rows.notes: Top title line. Most words legible but a few (e.g. 'impensis' and part of the dedication) are abbreviated and slightly faded; no amounts in this header line.
Short label immediately under the title; the first letter(s) are faint/abbreviated but context clearly indicates a 'pensioners' heading. No amounts visible on same line.

Entry text legible enough to read the name and a trailing '15' (probably an annuity/years). The small aligned numerical column for this specific line is faint and ambiguous in the image, so no numeric amount reliably extracted.
Name and 'Annue. 15' clear as another pensioner entry. Corresponding amount in the narrow pounds/shillings/pence columns is faint and not unambiguously readable for this row.
Third pensioner line. Description legible; amount column for this specific small group is not distinct enough to separate from adjacent small figures so left blank.

There is a small 'Sum' printed below the three pensioner lines with a numeric total to the right. The rightmost cluster is smudged; the reading '6 10 10' (6 pounds, 10 shillings, 10 pence) is the best consistent interpretation but the pence digit and adjacent marks are blurred.
Centered heading (Latinized) introducing the next block of festival/annual items. The heading is fairly clear though abbreviated; no amounts on this line.

rows.description: Ad fest: nativ. & augm. Southern & merton Stipend. et Officiarys
rows.notes: Description and amount align clearly. Right-hand columns show '23 00 00' (23 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence) with good pen strokes and unambiguous column alignment.

rows.description: Ad fest Ann. & augm. Red. Cudling? 3d & ttes Stipend
rows.notes: Text shows an annual/augment entry. Amount in right columns reads '17 13 04' clearly; a few letters in the place-name are uncertain (read as 'Cudling?'), hence 0.80 confidence.

rows.description: Ad fest Bapt. & 4th pte red. augment. Cudlingt. Stipend et Officiarys
rows.notes: Long entry for baptism/fourth part reduction & augment; amount column shows '47 02 08' aligned with this line. Handwriting of some words uncertain but numeric columns are well aligned and valid.

rows.description: Ad fest. mich. & augm. Red. Longwitn? Solatum & quinnear
rows.notes: This festival/michaelmas line corresponds to the next numeric line '29 06 08'. Some words in the description are abbreviated/obscure (e.g. 'Longwitn?') but the amount is clearly placed and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Solatium & qui near (continuation of prior entry?)
rows.notes: The line and amount '14 10 02' are visible; there is also a faint small character after the pence that looks like an 'ob' or loop indicating a halfpenny, so pence fraction interpreted as 0.5. Because the fractional mark is faint, confidence lowered to 0.60.
Amount '18 06 08' is clearly written in the pounds/shillings/pence columns for this entry. The description is a repeat formula 'Stipend et Officiarys' and legible.

rows.description: Ad fest mich. (another line: red. longwtn?)
rows.notes: The numeric entry '46 18 00' is clear and aligns with this descriptive line. Some of the descriptive words are abbreviated but do not affect numeric reading.

rows.description: Solatum & quinnear (final line of this festival block)
rows.notes: The amount '24 05 00' is clearly visible and aligned with this concluding entry of the group.
Subtotal for the festival block appears as a boxed/ruled total showing '221 02 07'. Alignment is clear and digits pass currency validation rules.
Small dated marginal note 'Novemb. 5th 1709' is legible; functions as a chronological header/annotation rather than an amount-bearing row.

Large final receipts total on the right reads '599 18 01' with a small fractional mark after the pence resembling a quarter (farthing) symbol; interpreted as 0.25. The numeric alignment is consistent though the fraction mark is small.

The payments total reads '415 02 11' and there is a small notation after the pence that appears like '3q' or 'qd' indicating three farthings (0.75). The fractional mark is less clear than the previous line, so confidence lowered.

rows.description: Sir Introduxit hoc anno Dis computans
rows.notes: This explanatory line states the difference introduced this year; the numeric '184 15 2' is clear and in proper ranges. Description words are slightly abbreviated but identifiable.
Signature/approval line. Name legible as 'Gul. Paynter R.' No amounts present.
Signature line; the surname is a little uncertain ('Verman' vs 'Vernon') but the 'Subrector' title is clear. No numeric values.
Signature is heavily abbreviated and partly struck through; read as 'Ric. Hurland' tentatively. No amounts.
Signature 'Sam: Adams' is clear and distinct. No numeric data.
A signature line appears but the name is heavily abbreviated/obscured by a blot; several letters unreadable so transcription is uncertain.
Final signature 'Robt. Rous.' is reasonably clear; no amounts on the line.
